prerequisites: A CoinGecko account, created at the pricing page (coingecko.com/en/api/pricing). A free Demo plan account is sufficient; paid plans (Basic, Analyst, Lite, Pro, Pro+, Enterprise) use the separate Pro API.
A CoinGecko API key generated from the Developer Dashboard (used as the api_key field). The key is optional for public access but authenticates requests when supplied.
The coin_id of the asset to extract, taken from CoinGecko's coin list (the /coins/list endpoint or the asset's page on coingecko.com).
A vs_currency target currency code supported by CoinGecko (for example usd), from the /simple/supported_vs_currencies endpoint.
A start_date (and optional end_date) for the historical range, given as an ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD) or UNIX timestamp.

authMethods.name: API key (Demo or Pro)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate by supplying your CoinGecko API key in the api_key field. CoinGecko accepts the key via the x-cg-demo-api-key header for free Demo plan keys, or the x-cg-pro-api-key header for paid Pro plan keys (query-string equivalents x_cg_demo_api_key / x_cg_pro_api_key also exist but are discouraged in production). The api_key field is optional, so the connector may also run unauthenticated against CoinGecko's public access.

setupSteps.title: Create a CoinGecko account
setupSteps.body: Sign up at https://www.coingecko.com/en/api/pricing. Choose the free Demo plan for unauthenticated or Demo-key access, or a paid plan (Basic, Analyst, Lite, Pro, Pro+, Enterprise) for higher limits and the Pro API.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Open the Developer Dashboard at https://www.coingecko.com/en/developers/dashboard and click '+ Add New Key'. Copy the generated key and provide it as the connector's api_key field. Leave api_key empty only if you intend to call CoinGecko's public endpoints without authentication.

setupSteps.title: Choose the coin and currency
setupSteps.body: Set coin_id to the CoinGecko asset identifier (look it up via the /coins/list endpoint or the coin's page on coingecko.com), and set vs_currency to a supported target currency code such as usd (see the /simple/supported_vs_currencies endpoint).

setupSteps.title: Define the historical date range
setupSteps.body: Set start_date (required) and optionally end_date to bound the historical market-chart range. Use an ISO date string in YYYY-MM-DD form or a UNIX timestamp. Set days to control the size of the requested window. These values map to the from/to parameters of CoinGecko's coin market chart range endpoint.
